title: IMD issues yellow warning for thunderstorms, heat wave and humid conditions across Odisha - Odisha TV

full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow warning for multiple districts across Odisha, forecasting a mix of thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds, heat wave, and humid conditions over the coming days due to an upper air cyclonic circulation persisting over North Coastal Odisha and its adjoining areas.
According to the latest weather bulletin, light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely to occur at isolated places in districts including Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Koraput, Rayagada, and Sundargarh during the initial phase of the forecast. The IMD has specifically warned that thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 40–50 kmph may occur in northern districts such as Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Bhadrak, and Balasore, while several other districts including Cuttack, Puri, Jagatsinghpur, and Khordha may experience winds of 30–40 kmph, particularly during afternoon and evening hours.
The weather department has also flagged heat wave conditions in parts of western Odisha, including Bolangir, Kalahandi, and Sambalpur, along with the possibility of warm night conditions in some of these regions. At the same time, hot and humid weather is expected to prevail across coastal and adjoining districts such as Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati, adding to discomfort levels.
In the subsequent days, thunderstorm activity is expected to gradually shift towards southern districts like Koraput, Rayagada, Malkangiri, Kandhamal, and Gajapati, while much of the rest of the state may witness relatively dry conditions. However, the IMD has cautioned that heat wave conditions are likely to intensify and expand to more districts including Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, Nuapada, Bargarh, Sonepur, and Boudh.
From Day 5 onwards, thunderstorm activity is expected to reduce significantly, remaining largely confined to southern pockets, while hot and humid conditions will continue to dominate coastal Odisha, and heat wave conditions may persist in interior districts.

The IMD has advised residents across Odisha to remain vigilant, avoid exposure during thunderstorms, and take necessary precautions against heat-related illnesses. People are urged to stay hydrated, limit outdoor activities during peak hours, and follow official advisories as the state braces for dynamic and potentially hazardous weather conditions over the next week.
